title: Prepend atmos dir if toml path is not found

body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
<!--- One sentence to describe the purpose of this PR, refer to any linked issues:
#14 -- this will link to issue 14
Closes #2 -- this will automatically close issue 2 on PR merge
-->
I think we should always use explicit microphysics for diagnostic EDMF updraft, because there are no prognostic variables? I wonder if this can fix the broken nightly AMIP: https://buildkite.com/clima/climacoupler-coarse-nightly-amip/builds/947#019d03d3-3f1b-448c-8673-f445a5ae5185

I saw that we are using explicit microphysics for prognostic EDMF 1M jobs, should we use it for diagnostic EDMF 1M too?

## To-do
<!---  list of proposed tasks in the PR, move to "Content" on completion 
- Proposed task
-->


## Content
<!---  specific tasks that are currently complete 
- Solution implemented
-->

- appropriate docstrings on all functions, structs, and modules, and included relevant documentation.

title: use explicit 1m microphysics for diagnostic EDMF updraft

title: Downgrade to 1.11

body: I'll wait for https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaAtmos.jl/pull/4361 to merge and then add the 1M kinematic driver to the CI as a microphysics reproducibility test
title: Fix kinematic driver
